blocks.text: `msix-sideload` and `msix-store` share local runtime responsibilities, but review and update boundaries differ.

rows: Signed MSIX with Store App plus local helper/broker under package identity
Explicit `background-helper` consent; Windows service install is disabled by default
Microsoft Store packaged desktop app plus full-trust helper/tray process
Store-managed updates; background helper requires consent and Store review approval

items: Provider CLIs are not included in MSIX packages.
`ExternalToolRegistry` preserves user-selected, env-override, and auto-detected provenance only.
Store App to helper control surface is Windows named pipe only; external TCP listener is forbidden.
App data and daemon state use package local data roots; user profile fallback is rejected.
Workspace access reaches C6 only when a Windows folder-picker grant and workspace consent are both active.
`msix-store` must support review/demo mode without provider installation.

blocks.text: `ResolveHelperRuntimePlan` computes MSIX roles and named-pipe local IPC, but does not spawn provider processes, open named pipes, or install Windows services.
